evidence_quotes.quote: I agree that it seems unfair when you purchase an online product and risk losing access to it due to the lack of end-of-life plans.

paragraphs.text: I came across this through the Stop Killing Games initiative videos. I believe this opinion isn't limited to games but extends to any online product as well. I agree that it seems unfair when you purchase an online product and risk losing access to it due to the lack of end-of-life plans. Since these products are only accessible online, discontinuing support can result in losing the product entirely. I think it's reasonable to request that the end of support for such a product be announced a few months in advance, accompanied by a brief FAQ on how to continue accessing it in a functional state. This could be through a download option or, in the case of a social experience, by designating an official third-party company permitted to host servers for the experience. Thank you for taking the time to consider my comment.
